verified.notes: Age requirement is 58+ (not 55, 60, or 65). Income limit is 50% of area median income (AMI) by household size, not a fixed dollar amount—varies by county and household composition. Program status confirmed active as of 2026.

programs.programName: Oregon Senior Free Tuition Audit Program

verified.notes: Program is tuition waiver for seniors 65+ to audit courses at community colleges per state statute; some colleges apply at 60 or 62 but official law is 65. No statewide phone found; contact individual colleges.

programs.notes: Program is tuition waiver for seniors 65+ to audit courses at community colleges per state statute; some colleges apply at 60 or 62 but official law is 65. No statewide phone found; contact individual colleges.

verified.notes: HRSN (Health-Related Social Needs) is not an age-based or means-tested program with income/asset limits. Eligibility is based on: (1) OHP membership status, (2) experiencing specific social needs (housing, nutrition, climate), and (3) experiencing one of six defined life transitions (e.g., homelessness, recent incarceration, child welfare involvement, IMD discharge, Medicaid-to-Medicare transition). No age, income, or asset thresholds apply. Official eligibility criteria available at https://www.oregon.gov/oha/hsd/medicaid-policy/pages/hrsn.aspx. No single statewide phone number for HRSN applications found in search results; applications are processed through individual Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) or 211info. Program is active as of April 2026 with authority extending to September 30, 2027.

No age, income, or asset thresholds apply. Official eligibility criteria available at https://www.oregon.gov/oha/hsd/medicaid-policy/pages/hrsn.aspx. No single statewide phone number for HRSN applications found in search results; applications are processed through individual Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) or 211info.
